entries.speaker: wynne
lines:
Valdris sends a knight and a spirit-speaker to ask
for our bows. That's new. Usually they send a letter.

entries.speaker: wynne
lines:
No. They aren't. Come up to the observatory. I want
to show you something.

entries.speaker: wynne
lines:
I've watched that haze grow for six months. It sits
where the ley lines meet. I don't need your scholars to tell me
what that means.

entries.speaker: lira
lines: The Convergence. Where the major ley lines intersect.

entries.speaker: wynne
lines:
The wind-spirits are agitated. They feel something
below the canopy that they won't name. When spirits refuse to
name a thing, you should be afraid.

entries.speaker: wynne
lines:
You want an alliance. Fine. But I don't ally with
weakness. Show me Valdris has a plan beyond "hope the ley lines
stabilize." Show me at the council, and you'll have Canopy
Reach's bows.

entries.speaker: lira
lines:
That's not weather. That's a resonance pattern. The
ley lines are being pulled toward a single point.

entries.speaker: maren
lines:
If something is drawing on the entire network toward
a single point deliberately—

entries.speaker: wynne
lines: Then we're all on a clock. And the clock is winning.
